title: Root tissue segmentation dataset
author: Wanner, Julian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The PHDFM dataset is composed of fluorescence microscopy images of root tissue samples from  A. thaliana , using the ratiometric fluorescent indicator 8‐hydroxypyrene‐1,3,6‐trisulfonic acid trisodium salt (HPTS). This semantic segmentation training dataset consists of 2D microscopy images (the brightfield channel for excitation at 405 nm), each containing a segmentation mask as an additional image channel (manually annotated by plant biologists). The segmentation masks classify pixels into the following 5 labels with the corresponding IDs: background (0), root tissue (1), early elongation zone (2), late elongation zone (3), and meristematic zone (4).

The segmentation masks classify pixels into the following 5 labels with the corresponding IDs: background (0), root tissue (1), early elongation zone (2), late elongation zone (3), and meristematic zone (4).
